member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls
member.summary: Runtime bisect toggles for the overlay perf harness, mutated from the perf overlay and read by UiBuilderService.Tick and ThemeRenderer. Only meaningful while — callers still read these unconditionally, but the overlay that flips them only exists when the harness is enabled, so production state stays at the inert defaults.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.MasterHudKill
member.summary: Skip drawing every registered window (run with the menu closed to prove the draw is the cost and rule out menu-induced game changes).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.ChromeKill
member.summary: Skip ThemeRenderer.DrawWindowChrome (isolate chrome cost).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.ForceOpaque
member.summary: Render large framework background fills opaque (isolate blend/overdraw).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.ThrottleN
member.summary: Draw window content only every Nth frame. 1 = every frame (off).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.IsMuted(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the named window is currently muted (skipped) by the perf harness.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.SetMuted(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Add or remove from the muted set; muted windows are skipped by isolation tests.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.MutedIds
member.summary: Snapshot of currently-muted window ids (overlay display).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.Uncap
member.summary: Set vSyncCount=0 + a very high targetFrameRate so the frame rate is CPU/GPU-bound rather than pinned to the display refresh — makes a per-frame CPU saving visible as an FPS delta instead of vsync-masked. Mutable so the Settings → Performance "Uncap Frame Rate" toggle re-applies vSync live (the host tick reconciles QualitySettings.vSyncCount to this value).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.UncapFromOverride
member.summary: True when UNCAP came from an explicit env var / flags-file entry (a dev/measurement override), so the persisted user setting must NOT clobber it at boot.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.UpdateRateHz
member.summary: Framework tick rate in Hz — how many times per second Stellar runs its whole per-frame work (services, HUD animation, plugin Updates, window interaction, input). The tick is driven by an InvokeRepeating schedule at this rate instead of every render frame, so most frames have ZERO managed-runtime entry — that entry is the ~12-18 fps "managed crossing" tax measured on this BepInEx 6 IL2CPP stack (a frame with no managed entry costs nothing; cost is linear in entries/sec). Lower = more game FPS (near-vanilla at 30); higher = smoother/lower-latency Stellar UI but more FPS cost. Mutable so the Settings → Performance slider re-rates the live ticker. Default 30 (near-vanilla FPS, ~33 ms UI latency). means "every frame" (Unity caps the schedule to the frame rate).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.RateFromOverride
member.summary: True when the rate came from an explicit env var / flags-file entry (a dev override), so the persisted user setting must NOT clobber it at boot.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.MinUpdateRateHz
member.summary: Slider floor — below this the HUD/input feel unusably laggy.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.MaxUpdateRateHz
member.summary: Slider ceiling — treated as "every frame" (the per-frame, max-cost mode).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.DefaultUpdateRateHz
member.summary: Default tick rate (near-vanilla FPS while keeping the HUD/input smooth).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.ClampRate(System.Int32)
member.summary: Clamp an arbitrary rate into the supported [, ] range. Shared by the env resolver and the Settings persistence.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.FlagValue(System.String)
member.summary: Read a numeric value flag: STELLAR_<NAME>=NN in env, or a line "NAME=NN" in stellar_perf.flags. Returns null if absent.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.Flag(System.String)
member.summary: Resolve a launch flag the same way as the perf flags: STELLAR_<NAME>=1 in the env, OR a line "NAME"/"NAME=1" in game_mini/stellar_perf.flags. Shared so other gates (e.g. ) read the deploy-script mode file without Heroic env edits.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfControls.IsThrottledOut(System.Int32)
member.summary: True when this frame should be SKIPPED under the throttle. Frame 0 and every Nth frame draw; the N-1 frames between are skipped. N<=1 disables.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe
member.summary:
Per-frame timing accumulator for the overlay perf harness. Gated by STELLAR_PERFHUD=1 (mirrors ): when disabled every method early-returns on a single cached field read so production pays nothing.
Frame model. OnGUI fires multiple times per rendered Unity frame (Layout, Repaint, input events). / and / accumulate into the current frame's running totals across every pass; is called exactly once per Unity frame (from the Host OnGUI sink's once-per-frame block) to (a) store the frame time, (b) commit the accumulated totals to the published , (c) reset the per-frame accumulators, and (d) bump .
Static mutable state is the sanctioned exception here — this is diagnostic infrastructure in the same category as , not business state.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.IsEnabled
member.summary: true when STELLAR_PERFHUD=1 (or =true) at startup.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.FrameCounter
member.summary: Monotonic Unity-frame counter; bumped by .

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.LogSink
member.summary: Optional log sink set by the host at boot; when non-null a per-interval summary line is written to the BepInEx log.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.EndDraw
member.summary: Mark the end of the OnGUI draw pass for the current frame; accumulated into the frame total.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.BeginUpdate
member.summary: Mark the start of the managed Update work for the current frame (plugins + services).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.EndUpdate
member.summary: Mark the end of the managed Update work; accumulated ms and managed alloc bytes into the frame total.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.BeginSeg(System.String)
member.summary: Start timing a named segment within the Update path (identifies per-plugin cost).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.EndSeg(System.String)
member.summary: Stop timing the named segment; accumulates into per-frame totals reported in the log summary.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.HookBegin
member.summary: Returns a start timestamp (ticks) for a hook, or 0 when disabled.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.HookBeginAlloc
member.summary: Per-thread allocated-bytes baseline for a hook (call on the hook's thread).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.HookEndCombat(System.Int64,System.Int64)

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.HookEndWire(System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Accumulate one wire-tap (TCP/UDP recv) invocation's time + this-thread allocation.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.PerfProbe.EndWindow(System.String)

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.StellarDiagnostics
member.summary:
Diagnostic mode toggle. Set environment variable STELLAR_DIAGNOSTICS=1 before launching the game (in Heroic's env-var settings) to enable per-event logs in PandaChatProbe, PandaWireTap, PandaCombatStubProbe, and ChatService. Default off so normal play produces minimal log volume.
The value is read once at process start and cached for the session — flipping the env var mid-run has no effect. This keeps the hot-path check a single field read (no repeated env lookups).
Lives in Stellar.Abstractions so every layer (Application, Infrastructure, Host) can gate on it without crossing the layered-dependency boundary.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Diagnostics.StellarDiagnostics.IsEnabled
member.summary: true when STELLAR_DIAGNOSTICS=1 (or =true, case-insensitive) was set in the process environment at startup. false otherwise.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.ChatMessage
member.summary: An incoming chat message delivered to plugins. MsgId is the server-assigned globally-unique id; plugins can use it to deduplicate when the same message arrives via multiple paths (live Notify + history Return). IsHistory is true for messages projected from the GetChipChatRecords reply that the server sends at login — plugins that react to live traffic (e.g. auto-reply, sound notifications) should skip history-replay messages by checking this flag.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId
member.summary: Strongly-typed wrapper around the int64 entity UUID used by the BPSR world server. The low 16 bits encode the entity type (640 = player, 64 / 32832 = monster); the upper bits encode the numeric uid.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId.#ctor(System.Int64)
member.summary: Strongly-typed wrapper around the int64 entity UUID used by the BPSR world server. The low 16 bits encode the entity type (640 = player, 64 / 32832 = monster); the upper bits encode the numeric uid.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId.None
member.summary: Sentinel representing an absent or unknown entity.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId.IsNone
member.summary: True when this id represents no entity (value zero).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId.IsPlayer
member.summary: True when the low 16 bits equal 640 (player entity-type marker).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId.IsMonster
member.summary: True when the low 16 bits equal 64 or 32832 (monster entity-type markers).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId.Uid
member.summary: Numeric uid extracted from the upper 48 bits of the full entity id.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityVitals
member.summary: Live HP snapshot for an entity, sourced from AttrCollection observations on the combat wire (AttrHp=11310, AttrMaxHp=11320). Available for every entity in AOI — players, mobs, NPCs — not just party members. Use to query.
member.param: Last-known current HP. Zero when the entity has never been observed.
Last-known max HP. Zero when the entity has never been observed or hasn't reported max yet.
True once at least one AttrHp or AttrMaxHp observation has landed.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityVitals.#ctor(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Live HP snapshot for an entity, sourced from AttrCollection observations on the combat wire (AttrHp=11310, AttrMaxHp=11320). Available for every entity in AOI — players, mobs, NPCs — not just party members. Use to query.
member.param: Last-known current HP. Zero when the entity has never been observed.
Last-known max HP. Zero when the entity has never been observed or hasn't reported max yet.
True once at least one AttrHp or AttrMaxHp observation has landed.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityVitals.Hp
member.summary: Last-known current HP. Zero when the entity has never been observed.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityVitals.MaxHp
member.summary: Last-known max HP. Zero when the entity has never been observed or hasn't reported max yet.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityVitals.IsKnown
member.summary: True once at least one AttrHp or AttrMaxHp observation has landed.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityVitals.Unknown
member.summary: Sentinel returned when no observation has been received for this entity yet.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle
member.summary: Visual chrome style for a registered plugin window. Passed via .

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.Party
member.summary: Party-panel chrome: opaque dark panel with accent border, for party/roster type content.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.Tracker
member.summary: Tracker chrome: semi-transparent panel for objective / quest tracker content.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.Borderless
member.summary: No chrome: transparent background, no border, no title bar — plugin draws its own content.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.Custom
member.summary: Custom chrome: plugin supplies all draw logic via its own element tree.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.HudOverlay
member.summary: Phase 9b HUD overlay chrome — fully transparent container. No box, no border, no title bar. Per-element treatment only: framework helpers (DrawHpBar / DrawCaption / DrawBody) consume the tokens (HudText, HudTextShadow, HudPillBg, HudBarBg) to draw a 1 px text drop-shadow and pill-chip + bar backgrounds inline. Used by PlayerHUD (pilot), CooldownBar, ChatTools.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.GlassMenu
member.summary: Phase 9b menu / tool-panel chrome — light/dark gradient backdrop (135° 3-stop linear, baked per preset to a 256×256 texture with rounded corners baked into the alpha channel). 1 px theme-tinted border, inline title text on the gradient, ✕ close glyph with MenuAccent hover, drag handle on the title bar minus the close hit area. Mirrors the game's Profile / Modules / Inventory mood. Used by Settings hub (post-9c) + DebugInfo / DataInspector / StatInspector / ModuleOptimizer in Phase 9c.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle.PillStatus
member.summary:
Phase 9b status-pill chrome — single-line rounded chip floating on the game world. ~14 px corner radius, padding 6/12 px, reuses
(shared with HudOverlay's level pill so the two chip-style chromes stay visually coherent). Whole-pill drag handle, no close button by design. Single-line content only — overflowing text spills outside the capsule background. Used by AutoNav nav-pill (post-9c).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowCategory
member.summary: Logical category for a plugin window, used to group windows in the Settings layout editor.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowCategory.HUD
member.summary: Gameplay heads-up displays (meters, cooldowns, party frames).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowCategory.Tools
member.summary: Interactive tool panels (module optimizer, data inspector, settings).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowCategory.Debug
member.summary: Developer / diagnostic windows (debug info, perf overlay).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.FrameworkVersion
member.summary: Single source of truth for the framework's version string. Lives in Abstractions so every layer (Host's BepInPlugin manifest, Infrastructure's AboutPanel, and any plugin that wants to gate behaviour on a particular framework release) can read the same constant without re-declaring it. Bump this on each user-visible release; Host's BootstrapPlugin.PluginVersion forwards to it so the BepInEx plugin manifest stays in lockstep.
member.remarks: BepInEx parses with SemVer semantics — it rejects trailing letters like 0.9.0a ("Skipping type because its version is invalid"). Use SemVer pre-release suffix syntax (0.9.0-alpha) so the chainloader accepts the manifest.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.FrameworkVersion.Value
member.summary: Current framework version. Phase 9a (Settings + native UI editor) lands as 0.9.0; plain SemVer (no pre-release suffix) keeps the BepInEx chainloader happy and reads as the first user-facing release-quality cut.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.AttributeInfo.NumType
member.summary: FightAttrTable.AttrNumType for this attribute's ×10 base: 0 = raw integer, 1 = per-10,000 percent (display value/100 with a % suffix), 2 = milliseconds (display value/1000 s). -1 when no FightAttr row matched — callers should fall back to a heuristic. -2 = catalogued non-stat attribute (identity / bookkeeping ids such as name or equip blobs — no displayable number).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.AttributeInfo.EnumName
member.summary: zproto EAttrType member name (e.g. "AttrCri"); empty when unknown.

member.summary: UI panel classification for an attribute, sourced from Bokura.ProfileAttrTableBase. is the raw classifier int (1=Offensive, 2=Defensive, 3=Support, 4=ElementalAttack, 5=ElementalBonus per observed game data — verify in iteration 1); is the localized group label.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.EnchantItemInfo
member.summary: One EquipEnchantItemTable row — a socketed gem at a specific level. Resolved from the wire's (enchant_item_type_id, enchant_level) pair: the wire level is an internal progression index, NOT the displayed level — the displayed level is baked into the gem ITEM's name ( → item-table name, e.g. "Crimson Foxen Sigil Lv.2").
member.param: Item id of the gem at this level; its item-table name carries the display level.
Flat stat grants of the gem (attr id + value), e.g. Haste +560. Never null.

member.param: Item id of the gem at this level; its item-table name carries the display level.

Lib-table selector for the basic libs: the FIRST element of the raw table column is a version marker, not a lib id (ZDPS-verified) — 1 = the normal EquipAttrLib table, 2 = the spec/school lib table (not loaded; spec-dependent values).
Attr-lib ids for the fixed basic attributes (version marker already stripped; typically one lib per breakthrough tier — index 0 = breakthrough 0).
Lib-table selector for the advanced libs (same semantics as ).

Attr-lib ids for the rolled advanced-attribute slots (version marker already stripped). Resolve each via IGameDataEquip.GetAttrLib(libId, EquipPart) — a lib id has one row per slot-part group, so the lookup is part-filtered.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ImagineCooldownCalc
member.summary:
Shared cooldown math for Battle-Imagine (and any multi-charge) skills, used by both the CooldownBar and the CombatMeter so their timing can't drift. Two parts:
— the real per-charge recharge: the wire duration (or valid_cd_time) reduced by cooldown reduction (every player has a flat ~10% default; gear/buffs add the cooldown-acceleration attrs 11960 skill / 11980 imagine). Haste (atk/cast speed) does NOT affect this.
— a sequential-recharge simulator. The wire moves begin only ON CAST and never reports a live charge count, and after the first charge's window expires it says nothing about the remaining charges still recharging. So we simulate: each cast (begin advances) spends one charge and re-anchors the soonest-charge window; charges then return one per perCharge; time-to-full counts down continuously.
One instance per consumer (it holds per-key recharge state). Unity-free and pure → unit-testable.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ImagineCooldownCalc.Recharge
member.summary: Result of for one key this tick.
member.param: True while not fully charged (show a tile); false when full (hide).
Milliseconds until ALL charges are back (counts down continuously across charges).
Inferred charges currently usable (0..max).
Progress toward full, 0..1 (1 = full) — for a fill bar / radial sweep.

member.summary: Effective per-charge recharge in ms: (validCdMs > 0 ? validCdMs : durMs) × (1 − cdReductionFraction).
member.param: Wire duration (nominal per-charge recharge).
Wire valid_cd_time when populated (else 0 → falls back to ).
Total cooldown reduction, 0..1 (e.g. 0.10 default + attr/10000).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ImagineInfo
member.summary: Static data for one Battle Imagine (Resonance Skill): display + cooldown/charge model.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ProfessionSpecs
member.summary:
Static skill-id → sub-profession (spec) knowledge. There is no wire field for spec — it is derived from the skills a player has equipped (the AOI AttrSkillLevelIdList loadout carries each spec's signature skills, so the spec is known the moment a player appears, before any combat) or, as a fallback, from the skills they cast. Mapping ported from the BPSR-ZDPS reference (Professions.GetSubProfessionIdBySkillId).
Sub-profession ids use the format <ProfessionId>_00_<SpecIndex> as decimal: 01_00_01 = 10001 (Stormblade / Iaido), 02_00_01 = 20001 (FrostMage / Icicle), etc.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ProfessionSpecs.SubProfessionFromSkill(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the sub-profession id for the given skill id, or null if the skill id is not associated with any known spec (including skill id 0).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ProfessionSpecs.FromLoadout(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.SkillLevel})
member.summary: Resolves the spec from an equipped-skill loadout (first signature-skill match), or null when no loadout skill maps to a spec. Pre-combat: works the moment the AOI broadcast delivers the loadout.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ProfessionSpecs.Name(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the English display name for the given sub-profession id, or null if the id is not recognised (including 0 / Unknown).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.GameData.ProfessionSpecs.TalentSchool(System.Int32)
member.summary: Resolves the talent-school id (for school-lib gear-attr lookups) from a player's SPEC. Returns 0 when the spec is unknown — the school gear-attr table is keyed by spec ids (104=Icicle …), NOT base-profession ids, so a base-profession fallback matched nothing and produced a silently-empty Advanced section (in-world 2026-06-13). 0 lets callers show an honest "spec unknown" message.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquippedSet

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult
member.summary: Outcome of an dispatch. Maps the game's EErrorCode reply to plugin-friendly cases — plugins should never need to read raw error codes.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.Success
member.summary: Game accepted the request; matching event fired.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.GameApiUnavailable
member.summary: Reflection couldn't resolve the game's Lua bridge (ZLuaFramework / ModVM). Typically appears at boot before HybridCLR finishes loading.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.PlayerNotInWorld
member.summary: No live player entity (character select / loading screen).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.SlotLocked
member.summary: Game returned slot-not-unlocked (Tips lang 1042105).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.SlotConflict
member.summary: Game returned mod-type conflict or category cap exceeded (Tips lang 1042106 / 1042110).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.SlotEmpty
member.summary: Returned by when the target slot was already empty. Not an error.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.ModuleNotInInventory
member.summary: The supplied module UUID isn't in the player's current inventory snapshot.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.RpcError
member.summary: Server-side EErrorCode was non-zero but didn't match any specific case.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.Timeout
member.summary: 6 seconds elapsed without the matching event firing (matches the Lua coro_util.async_to_sync(..., 6) timeout).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.EquipResult.Cancelled
member.summary: Caller's CancellationToken fired before completion.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.GearAttrRoll
member.summary: One ROLLED attribute slot on an owned gear piece. The wire stores the roll as an EquipAttrLibTable ROW id plus a 0–100 roll PERCENTILE — not an attribute id/value pair. Resolve via IGameDataEquip.GetAttrLibRow(LibRowId): each returned entry's displayed stat is floor(Percentile * (Max - Min) / 100 + Min) (the game's own formula in equip_attr_parse_vm.lua; verified against the live gear sheet 2026-06-12).
member.param: EquipAttrLibTable row id (the wire roll-map key).
Roll quality 0–100; 100 = the entry's Max.

True when the roll came from EquipAttr.equip_attr_set (the current spec's set): its row id keys the v2 SCHOOL lib table (GetSchoolAttrLibRow), not the v1 table. Resolving it against v1 returned a colliding row's wrong attrs (in-world 2026-06-13).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Inventory.GearAttrRoll.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: One ROLLED attribute slot on an owned gear piece. The wire stores the roll as an EquipAttrLibTable ROW id plus a 0–100 roll PERCENTILE — not an attribute id/value pair. Resolve via IGameDataEquip.GetAttrLibRow(LibRowId): each returned entry's displayed stat is floor(Percentile * (Max - Min) / 100 + Min) (the game's own formula in equip_attr_parse_vm.lua; verified against the live gear sheet 2026-06-12).
member.param: EquipAttrLibTable row id (the wire roll-map key).
Roll quality 0–100; 100 = the entry's Max.

True when the roll came from EquipAttr.equip_attr_set (the current spec's set): its row id keys the v2 SCHOOL lib table (GetSchoolAttrLibRow), not the v1 table. Resolving it against v1 returned a colliding row's wrong attrs (in-world 2026-06-13).

member.summary: Roll quality 0–100; 100 = the entry's Max.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.PartyMember
member.summary: One party member's snapshot. Composed from wire deliveries on the GrpcTeamNtf service. Immutable; rebuilt by PartyService whenever any field changes.

remarks.para: CharId — Wire char_id. Also EntityId.Uid under the Phase 4 identity assumption.
Name — TeamMemberSocialData.basic_data.name. null until first social sync arrives.
Profession — Raw profession id from TeamMemberSocialData.profession_data. Phase 5 IGameData will translate to a name.
Level — Character level from TeamMemberSocialData.basic_data.
Hp — Live HP from TeamMemberFastSyncData.
MaxHp — Maximum HP from TeamMemberFastSyncData.
SceneId — Scene id from fast-sync. When this differs from the local scene, the member is out-of-AOI but HP is still tracked via wire.
Position — World-space position of the member.
IsOnline — TeamMemData.online_status == 1.
IsSelf — True when CharId matches the local player. False until ICombatSnapshot.LocalEntityId becomes non-None — known v1 quirk if joining a party before first combat.
GroupId — TeamMemData.group_id. 0 in non-raid parties; meaningful only when PartyType == Raid20.

Slot — 0-based position WITHIN the group (the member's order in the team's slot list), from NotifyTeamGroupUpdate (TeamMemberGroupInfo.char_ids ordering). -1 when not yet known. Lets the raid grid place each member at their exact Team×Slot instead of roster order.

DPS aggregation — query ICombatLookup.GetLiveDps(member.EntityId) instead of a per-member DPS field. The combat service aggregates per source EntityId for every entity in AOI, so the same call works for party members, mobs, and randoms uniformly.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.PartyMember.#ctor(System.Int64,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Int32,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.Position3D,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: One party member's snapshot. Composed from wire deliveries on the GrpcTeamNtf service. Immutable; rebuilt by PartyService whenever any field changes.

remarks.para: CharId — Wire char_id. Also EntityId.Uid under the Phase 4 identity assumption.
Name — TeamMemberSocialData.basic_data.name. null until first social sync arrives.
Profession — Raw profession id from TeamMemberSocialData.profession_data. Phase 5 IGameData will translate to a name.
Level — Character level from TeamMemberSocialData.basic_data.
Hp — Live HP from TeamMemberFastSyncData.
MaxHp — Maximum HP from TeamMemberFastSyncData.
SceneId — Scene id from fast-sync. When this differs from the local scene, the member is out-of-AOI but HP is still tracked via wire.
Position — World-space position of the member.
IsOnline — TeamMemData.online_status == 1.
IsSelf — True when CharId matches the local player. False until ICombatSnapshot.LocalEntityId becomes non-None — known v1 quirk if joining a party before first combat.
GroupId — TeamMemData.group_id. 0 in non-raid parties; meaningful only when PartyType == Raid20.

Slot — 0-based position WITHIN the group (the member's order in the team's slot list), from NotifyTeamGroupUpdate (TeamMemberGroupInfo.char_ids ordering). -1 when not yet known. Lets the raid grid place each member at their exact Team×Slot instead of roster order.

DPS aggregation — query ICombatLookup.GetLiveDps(member.EntityId) instead of a per-member DPS field. The combat service aggregates per source EntityId for every entity in AOI, so the same call works for party members, mobs, and randoms uniformly.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.SkillLevel
member.summary: One entry of an entity's equipped skill loadout, decoded from the combat wire's AttrSkillLevelIdList attribute (EAttrType=116). The server broadcasts this per-entity for every player in AOI, so it is the canonical source for a player's equipped skills — including Battle Imagines (skills whose SlotPositionId contains 7 or 8).
member.param: The equipped skill id (proto skill_id=1).
Current level of the skill (proto current_level=2).
Remodel level / tier of the skill (proto remodel_level=3).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec
member.summary: Immutable declaration of a plugin window's identity, initial geometry, and chrome options. Passed to to create the window.
member.param: Stable string id, unique per plugin. Used to persist position and hotkey binding.
Display title shown in the title bar and Settings layout editor.
Initial position and size applied on first run (before user adjustments are persisted).
Logical category that determines which group this window appears in within the layout editor.
Visual chrome style applied to the window frame.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.#ctor(System.String,System.String,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowRect,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowCategory,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowPanelStyle)
member.summary: Immutable declaration of a plugin window's identity, initial geometry, and chrome options. Passed to to create the window.
member.param: Stable string id, unique per plugin. Used to persist position and hotkey binding.
Display title shown in the title bar and Settings layout editor.
Initial position and size applied on first run (before user adjustments are persisted).
Logical category that determines which group this window appears in within the layout editor.
Visual chrome style applied to the window frame.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Id
member.summary: Stable string id, unique per plugin. Used to persist position and hotkey binding.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Title
member.summary: Display title shown in the title bar and Settings layout editor.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.DefaultRect
member.summary: Initial position and size applied on first run (before user adjustments are persisted).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Category
member.summary: Logical category that determines which group this window appears in within the layout editor.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Style
member.summary: Visual chrome style applied to the window frame.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.StartVisible
member.summary: Whether the window is visible on first run (before user toggles via hotkey).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Draggable
member.summary: When true the window is a movable dialog: drag-by-title-bar (the post-drag rect is committed + persisted) and excluded from the Shift+` Layout editor (it owns its own position). When false the window is positioned via the Layout editor and any title-bar drag is discarded. Defaults false. Settings windows + opt-in plugin panels (e.g. StatInspector settings) set this true.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Closable
member.summary: When true the chrome draws a ✕ close glyph that hides the window. Defaults false (plugin windows manage their own visibility). Independent of so a window can be draggable without a close button (e.g. the Settings hub).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.AutoHideBehindGameMenus
member.summary: When true the framework stops DRAWING this window while a full-screen game menu is open (it reappears on close), so combat HUDs behave like the native HUD instead of floating over menus. Does NOT change the user's Show/Hide state — purely a draw-time suppression. Defaults false.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.HideUntilInWorld
member.summary: When true the framework stops DRAWING this window until the player is logged in / in-world, so gameplay HUDs don't float over the title / character-select screens. Reappears once logged in. Purely draw-time suppression (does NOT change Show/Hide state). Defaults false — debug tools (DebugInfo, AutoNav) leave it off so they stay visible pre-login.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.AutoSizeWidth
member.summary: Content-size the window WIDTH to its body instead of fixing it to .Width. Only safe for windows without wrapping text (e.g. the launcher's fixed-width icon tiles) — the in-world clip bug that forced fixed width was a wrapping-text problem the launcher does not have. Defaults false.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.EditModeDragOnly
member.summary: When true the chrome draws a bottom-right ↘ resize grip; dragging it changes the window size (clamped to / .. /), and the new size persists alongside the position. The window's vertical content-fit is disabled (fixed height); a ScrollElement in the body fills the freed space. Defaults false. The CombatMeter list uses this.

The window's drag mode, honoured by EVERY chrome (decoupled from chrome style): false (default) → free-drag any time, like a popup dialog (Settings, DataInspector, CombatMeter History); true → "pinned": moves only while layout edit-mode (Shift+`) is active, and the editor draws its movable rect — for gameplay HUD overlays (CombatMeter meter, AutoNav) that shouldn't move during play.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.Resizable
member.summary: When true the chrome draws a resize grip; dragging it changes the window size (clamped to Min/Max bounds).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.MinWidth
member.summary: Minimum allowed window width in pixels when is true.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.MinHeight
member.summary: Minimum allowed window height in pixels when is true.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.MaxWidth
member.summary: Maximum allowed window width in pixels when is true.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.MaxHeight
member.summary: Maximum allowed window height in pixels when is true.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec.ShowTitleBar
member.summary: GlassMenu only: draw the top title bar. Defaults true. Set false for windows that self-compose their own header inside the body (the launcher, whose header is top in Full/vertical but a LEFT strip in horizontal — a single fixed top bar can't express both). With no title bar the whole frame becomes the drag handle (if ), and the body must supply its own close affordance.

member.summary: Align text to the left of the cell.

member.summary: Centre text horizontally in the cell.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.TextElement
member.summary: Themed text. Func null (or returns null) = framework default; a Func lets colour animate per-refresh (e.g. delta-flash). > 0 fixes the cell width (the text wraps within it) — use to form aligned columns (e.g. a plugin-name column so the version after it starts at a consistent x). sets horizontal alignment (Right for numeric columns). draws a dark outline behind the glyphs — for chrome-less overlays (a borderless HUD with no background) where light text must stay legible over arbitrary world backgrounds.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ConditionalElement
member.summary: React-style conditional. Both subtrees are built once; the renderer SetActive-toggles them each refresh from (no reconciliation). may be null. = the active branch expands to fill leftover height in a fixed-size (Resizable) window (so e.g. a meter's list scroll grows with the window). Default false — no effect in content-sized windows.

Default false — no effect in content-sized windows.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ListElement
member.summary: Variable-length list, bounded by Slots.Count. All slots built once; the first () are SetActive-shown each refresh. >1 grids them. / (when > 0, multi-column only) override the grid's default cell size — use to widen columns past the framework default (e.g. the StatInspector mini-HUD, whose icon+label+value row needs more than the default cell width).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChat.Send(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.ChatTarget,System.String)
member.summary: Sends to the specified channel. Fire-and-forget; failures are logged, never thrown.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle
member.summary: User-selected global chrome control styles — the button + scrollbar look applied to every GlassMenu panel. Persisted with the theme; changing either fires the theme's so the renderer rebuilds its skin. A plugin may pin its own per-window button and scrollbar style via , which the user cannot override here.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.ButtonStyle
member.summary: Currently active button visual style applied to all GlassMenu panels.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.ScrollbarStyle
member.summary: Currently active scrollbar visual style applied to all GlassMenu panels.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.WindowOpacity
member.summary: Body opacity (0..1) of the native uGUI window chrome for the ACTIVE theme. Persisted per-preset so e.g. Default can stay translucent/frosted while Dark/Light read as near-opaque over bright in-world scenes. A custom theme inherits its base preset's opacity.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.FontScale
member.summary: Global font scale (0.8–1.4). Same value as (the concrete theme implements both) — exposed here so the uGUI window renderer can scale its text without taking a second INamedTheme dependency.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.SetButtonStyle(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.MenuButtonStyle)
member.summary: Changes the global button style and persists it with the active theme preset.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.SetScrollbarStyle(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.MenuScrollbarStyle)
member.summary: Changes the global scrollbar style and persists it with the active theme preset.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IChromeStyle.SetWindowOpacity(System.Single)
member.summary: Set for the active theme's preset (clamped to [0.3, 1]). Persists + fires so the window chrome rebakes.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IClientState

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IClientState.IsLoggedIn

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IClientState.CurrentSceneName
member.summary: Identifier (currently a numeric scene id, not a friendly name) for the active scene.

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IClientState.Login
member.summary: Fired once when the player finishes loading into the world (in-world ready).

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IClientState.Logout
member.summary: Fired once when the player disconnects or returns to character select.

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IClientState.SceneChanged
member.summary: Fired when the active scene changes. Argument is the new scene identifier, or null when no scene is active.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IColorRegistry
member.summary: Plugins register the colours they OWN. Key is namespaced "Owner.Concept.Property" (e.g. "PlayerHUD.StaminaBar.Fill"), unique. A Register overload supplies the owner's colour for each built-in preset. Register only colours a plugin owns; to match a theme colour, read Theme.Colors.X directly instead.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IColorRegistry.Register(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.ThemePreset,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.ColorRgba})
member.summary: Registers a color slot with per-preset defaults.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IColorRegistry.Register(System.String,System.String,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.ColorRgba)
member.summary: Registers a color slot whose default is the same for every theme preset. Convenience over the per-preset dictionary overload.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IColorRegistry.Unregister(System.String)
member.summary: Unregisters the color slot identified by .

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IColorSlot
member.summary:
Handle returned by 's Register methods. Read when drawing — it resolves to the colour for the active theme, honouring user overrides. Cache the handle, not the value.
Disposing a slot unregisters it from the owning .

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IColorSlot.Value
member.summary: Resolves the color for the currently active theme preset, honouring user overrides.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatEvents
member.summary: Combat event stream. Mirrors the event half of the original mixed combat surface; polled state lives on and per-entity lookups on . Events always fire on the Unity main thread (drained once per Game.Update postfix).

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatEvents.CombatEventOccurred
member.summary: Fires on the main (Unity) thread once per game tick during drain.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup
member.summary: Per-entity combat lookups. Covers any entity observed in AOI on the combat wire — not limited to party members or the local player. Mirrors the lookup half of the original mixed combat surface; the polled local snapshot lives on and the event stream lives on .

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.BuffsFor(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Active buffs on the given entity, derived from observed BuffChanged events. Empty if entity is unknown.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetEntityName(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Resolved display name for an entity, or null if unknown.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetVitals(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Last-known HP / MaxHP snapshot for an entity from the combat wire's AttrCollection stream. Returns for entities not yet observed. Works for every entity in AOI — not just party members.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetLiveDps(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: 5-second sliding-window damage-per-second for a damage source (). Returns 0 for entities that have never been observed as a damage source, or when the source's last hit fell out of the 5-second window. Aggregated automatically as events flow through — no per-plugin tracking needed.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetLiveHps(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: 5-second sliding-window healing-per-second for a heal source ( with IsHeal=true). Returns 0 for entities that have never been observed as a heal source, or when the source's last heal fell out of the 5-second window. Aggregated automatically as heal events flow through — no per-plugin tracking needed.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetTeamId(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Last-known team id for an entity from the AttrTeamId (id=194) attribute on the combat wire. Returns 0 when the entity is solo, has never been observed, or is out-of-AOI. Two entities with the same non-zero team id are teammates. The cleanest way to identify in-AOI party members — does not require decoding the GrpcTeamNtf service.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetFightPoint(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Last-known ability/combat score for an entity from the AttrFightPoint (id=10030) attribute on the combat wire (the "Ability Score" ZDPS shows). Returns 0 when never observed / out-of-AOI. Present per-entity for player (EntChar) entities in SyncNearEntities, so it's available for the local player and visible party members alike.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatLookup.GetSkillLevels(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Last-known equipped skill loadout for an entity, decoded from the combat wire's AttrSkillLevelIdList attribute (EAttrType=116). Broadcast per-entity for every player in AOI. Returns an empty list when the entity has never reported its loadout. Each entry carries skill id, current level, and tier; callers identify Battle Imagines by checking the skill's SlotPositionId against the skill table.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot
member.summary: Polled snapshot of the local player's combat state plus a ring buffer of recently observed events. Mirrors the polled half of the original mixed combat surface; the event stream lives on and per-entity lookups live on . All reads are safe from the Unity main thread.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot.IsAvailable
member.summary: True once the wire tap has seen the first SyncToMeDeltaInfo after entering world.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot.LocalEntityId
member.summary: Local player's entity UUID. until first SyncToMeDeltaInfo.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot.LocalCooldowns
member.summary: Local player's current skill cooldowns. Empty out of combat / pre-login.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot.LocalBuffs
member.summary: Active buffs on the local player.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot.ServerNowMs
member.summary: Latest server epoch (ms) seen on the SyncServerTime notify. Zero until first observation.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ICombatSnapshot.RecentEvents
member.summary: Ring buffer of recent events (capacity 500).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IConfigSection
member.summary:
A named subset of a plugin's configuration. Read access via is lock-free against the in-memory cache; writes via are cached and persisted to disk on .
Supported T: primitives (, , , , , ), arrays of primitives, and string-keyed dictionaries of primitives. Records and complex objects are out of scope for v1 — use multiple keys instead.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IConfigSection.Get``1(System.String,``0)
member.summary: Returns the value stored at , or if the key is absent or the stored value can't be coerced to . Never throws.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IConfigSection.Set``1(System.String,``0)
member.summary: Stores at in the in-memory cache. Does NOT write to disk — caller must invoke when batch of edits is complete.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IConfigSection.Save
member.summary: Flushes the in-memory cache for this section to disk, then fires . Cross-process / external-edit detection is handled by the framework — plugins don't poll the file.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IConfigSection.SaveQuiet
member.summary: Persists the section without raising — for echo-suppression when the writer is reacting to its own change.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityContextMenu.ItemsFor(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.EquippedItem.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: One equipped item resolved for display: which item id occupies which slot.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityDetail
member.summary: Per-entity detail captured from the combat wire's SyncNearEntities attribute stream — the full broadcast attribute map and equipment for any in-AOI entity. Identity/skills come from ; derived self-only stats from .

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityDetail.GetAttributes(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: The entity's full broadcast numeric attribute map (attr id → value); empty if unknown.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityDetail.GetEquipment(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: The entity's equipped items (slot + item id); empty if not broadcast.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityDetail.GetFashion(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Worn cosmetics for an entity from the broadcast AttrFashionData attribute, with actual dye colours. Empty for entities that never reported fashion / out of AOI.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityDetail.GetSocialSnapshot(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: The player's last on-demand social-data reply (identity + ability score + profession + gear + wardrobe), or null if none received. Available for any player; the inspector's fallback when the AOI broadcast is absent (far/never-seen players). See .

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait
member.summary: Live, posed 3D portrait of a player entity — full body, real outfit, idle-animated — rendered by the game's own UI-model pipeline into a render texture that a displays. Works for the local player and any other player (the subject is created from the player's social data, the same way the game's own character preview windows do it). The plugin calls when its portrait slot opens on an entity and reads (the boxed UnityEngine.Texture) to display it.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.IsActive
member.summary: True while a portrait subject is active (model created or being created).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.Show(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.EntityId)
member.summary: Show the portrait for an entity. Non-player ids hide the portrait instead. The model is created asynchronously — stays null for a few frames until the game delivers it.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.Hide
member.summary: Hide the portrait and release the model back to the game's pool.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.Texture
member.summary: The boxed UnityEngine.Texture to display, or null while inactive / still loading.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.Orbit(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Rotate the portrait subject. / are pointer-drag deltas in pixels (horizontal drag spins the model; vertical drag is currently ignored).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.Zoom(System.Single)
member.summary: Zoom the portrait view. Positive moves closer (e.g. scroll-wheel up).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.Pan(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Pan the portrait camera (shift+drag). / are pointer-drag deltas in pixels; the look-at point slides so different parts of the model can be framed.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IEntityPortrait.SetViewport(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Report the display box's current pixel size so the portrait sizes its render texture + projection to match — it then fills the (resizable) pane top-to-bottom with no letterbox or stretch.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IFramework
member.summary: Per-frame callbacks driven by the game's main update loop.

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IFramework.Update
member.summary: Fired once per game frame. Argument is deltaTime in seconds.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IFramework.FrameCount
member.summary: Monotonic frame counter incremented before each dispatch.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameAssets
member.summary: Toolkit service for loading game assets (textures, sprites) via the game's own Addressables loader (ZResLoader). Provides an opaque texture handle so plugins can render icons without a direct Unity dependency on Texture2D.
member.remarks: Loading is asynchronous (driven by the game's UniTask Addressables pipeline). Call every frame from an Update tick; it returns null while the load is in progress and the resolved texture handle once the load succeeds. A permanently failed load also returns null — the caller should fall back to text-only rendering.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameAssets.LoadProfessionIcon(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the atlas texture handle for the profession icon identified by , kicking off an async Addressables load on the first call. Returns null while loading, on failure, or when no profession row / icon path is available for the given id.
member.param: The numeric profession id (1–11 for known classes).
member.returns: An opaque UnityEngine.Texture2D handle (safe to cast to UnityEngine.Texture for rendering) or null. The UV sub-rect for the icon's region within the atlas is returned via .

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameAssets.LoadProfessionIcon(System.Int32,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.UvRect@)
member.summary: Same as but also returns the normalised UV rect ( — 0..1 range, bottom-left origin, UV texture-space convention) for the icon's sub-region within the atlas. Pass the handle and UV to a or MeterRowData.CrestTexture / CrestUv pair.
member.param: The numeric profession id (1–11 for known classes).
The UV sub-rect for the icon. Set to (0,0,1,1) (full texture) when the texture is not yet available or the load failed.

member.param: The Battle Imagine skill id (from the entity's equipped skill loadout).
The UV sub-rect for the icon. Set to (0,0,1,1) (full texture) when the texture is not yet available or the load failed.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameAssets.LoadItemIcon(System.Int32,Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.UvRect@)
member.summary: Atlas/texture handle for an item's inventory icon, async-loaded via ZResLoader using the item row's icon path. Returns null while loading, on failure, or when the item/icon is unknown; is the icon's sub-rect ((0,0,1,1) until resolved).
member.param: The item id (gear pieces share ids with their ItemTable rows).
The UV sub-rect for the icon within its atlas, or full-rect when standalone.

member.param: The skill id (from the entity's skill loadout).
The UV sub-rect for the icon within its atlas, or full-rect when standalone.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameData
member.summary:
Read-only lookup over the game's static Panda.Table data. Resolves Bokura.*TableBase row IDs to typed POCO records with display strings pre-resolved through the game's LocalizationMgr.
Eager batch (Skill, Buff, Profession, Attribute, Item) is loaded inline at HybridCLR-ready; becomes true once that completes. 17 deferred tables drain one per Game.Update tick — lookups return null until their table is loaded (typically a few seconds after IsAvailable=true).
Strings are resolved at cache-build time. Mid-run language switch will not refresh names until next game restart (deferred to Phase 6+).

member.summary: World sub-table: maps, scenes, NPCs, monsters.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataEquip
member.summary: Static-data lookups for the gear-slot tables (equip rows, attr-lib ranges, slot names). Deferred-loaded: lookups return null/empty for a few seconds after boot.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataEquip.GetEquipRow(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the gear-piece row for , or null if unknown / not yet loaded.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataEquip.GetAttrLib(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the attribute entries (with value ranges) for an attr-lib id, filtered to the row whose allow-part list contains (a lib id has one table row per slot-part group — an unfiltered merge mixes other slots' ranges in; ZDPS-verified semantics). Empty list when unknown / not yet loaded.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataEquip.GetSchoolAttrLib(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the v2 SCHOOL attr-lib entries for an attr-lib id, filtered to the row whose allow-part list contains AND whose talent-school list contains (resolve via ProfessionSpecs.TalentSchool). This is the spec-dependent advanced-roll source for raid/v2 gear. Empty when unknown / not yet loaded.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataEquip.GetAttrLibRow(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the attribute entries for an attr-lib table ROW id, or an empty list. The wire's per-instance roll maps (EquipAttr.basic_attr etc.) key by row id with a 0–100 roll percentile value: displayed stat = floor(pct * (Max - Min) / 100 + Min) per entry (verified against the game's own equip_attr_parse_vm.lua, 2026-06-12).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataEquip.GetSchoolAttrLibRow(System.Int32)
member.summary: Like but resolves against the v2 SCHOOL lib table — use for rolls flagged (from equip_attr_set). Kept separate because a school row id can collide with a v1 row id.

member.summary: Returns the localized slot label for an code, or null.

member.summary: Resolves a socketed gem from the wire's (enchant_item_type_id, enchant_level) pair to its (gem item id whose name carries the display level + flat effects). Null if unknown / not yet loaded.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IGameDataInventory.GetEquip(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Returns the equip-enchant-item row for , or null if unknown.
v1 scope warning: on the current build this lookup is backed by Bokura.EquipEnchantItemTableBase (222 rows of enchant-item metadata). It does NOT return gear-piece info (helms, gloves, armour slots, etc.) — those rows live as items in with / . Calling GetEquip(helmet_id) will return null. Recon for a separate gear-slot table is tracked for Phase 7 (ModuleOptimizer); this signature is preserved so the v2 source can be swapped in without a breaking change.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IInventory
member.summary:
Read-only access to the player's module-package inventory and currently equipped set. Sampled at 1Hz from the Game.Update tick; not subscription driven (inventory changes are infrequent enough that 1Hz polling beats the bookkeeping cost of subscriptions).
Pair with to resolve ModulePart.AttrId labels (Phase 6 reuse).
Threading: and are thread-safe lock-free reads (Volatile under the hood). The event raises on the framework Update thread; subscribers can call IMGUI from the handler.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IInventory.IsAvailable
member.summary: True after the first successful probe sample. False during character select / loading / before HybridCLR finishes.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IInventory.GetModules
member.summary: Current module inventory snapshot, or null until the first sample lands.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IInventory.GetEquipped
member.summary: Currently equipped module UUIDs by slot (1..4). Slots with no module are absent from the returned dictionary.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IInventory.GetSelfGear
member.summary: The LOCAL player's currently equipped gear instances with their per-piece rolled attributes, ordered by slot. Empty (never null) until the first full container sync lands. Staleness contract: the list is refreshed on full container syncs (login / map change); mid-session refines or re-rolls may be stale until the next full sync (method-22 incremental deltas are not decoded for gear). Thread-safe lock-free read, like the other getters.

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IInventory.InventoryChanged
member.summary: Fires once when the snapshot or equipped set diffs the previous tick. Polling cadence is 1Hz so the event fires at most once per second.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IModuleEquip
member.summary:
Game-state mutation primitive: equip / uninstall modules via the game's own RPC dispatcher. Implementations invoke the game's Lua functions ModVM.AsyncEquipMod / ModVM.AsyncUninstallMod through the ZLuaFramework bridge — the plugin supplies inputs, the game's Lua code builds the protobuf and applies its own validation.
User-initiated only. Plugins MUST trigger calls from a user action (button press, slash command, hotkey) — not from a timer, scheduled event, or background loop. See the project's out-of-scope policy (README).
Async: every call polls the game's Mod.ModSlots map until it reflects the requested change, or times out at 6 seconds (matching the Lua proxy timeout). Callers must await and check the returned .

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IModuleEquip.IsAvailable
member.summary: True when the game's RPC dispatcher has been resolved AND a real player entity is in-world. False during boot, character select, or zone transition.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IModuleEquip.InstallAsync(System.Int32,System.Int64,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Invokes ModVM.AsyncEquipMod(moduleUuid, slotId). Slot is 1..4 (game enforces ModSlotMaxCount = 4). The Task completes when the game's Mod.ModSlots map reflects the equip or the 6-second timeout elapses.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IModuleEquip.UninstallAsync(System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Invokes ModVM.AsyncUninstallMod(slotId). Returns (not an error) if the slot held no module.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPartyControl
member.summary: Plugin-facing control over the local player's party. Actions are routed through the game's own dispatcher (never hand-built packets); the game applies its own validation, so a request may be silently rejected (e.g. a non-leader, or the 20-player mode being locked for the account).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPartyControl.IsAvailable
member.summary: True once the game-side team bridge has resolved and a request can be issued.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPartyControl.SetMemberType(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.PartyType)
member.summary: Requests the game switch the current party between 5-player () and 20-player raid (). Must be called on the main thread, from a user-initiated command. No-op for , when the requested size already matches, or when not the party leader. The party's activity target is preserved. The result is observed via once the server broadcasts it.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPartyControl.MoveMember(System.Int64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves to raid (1–4) at (0–4) via the game's own AsyncUpdateTeamGroup dispatcher — never a hand-built packet. Must be called on the main thread from a user-initiated command. Leader-only and 20-player-raid-only; the game validates and may silently reject. No-op when the team bridge is unresolved. The result is observed via the party roster's group/slot once the server broadcasts it.
member.param: Character id of the member to move.
Destination raid group, 1-based (1–4).
Destination slot within the group, 0-based (0–4).

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerIdentity.Profession
member.summary: Primary profession id; zero when not yet loaded.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerVitals
member.summary: Vitals (HP / Stamina) facet of the local player's state.
member.remarks: Phase 9a supplement renamed Mana → Stamina to match Star Resonance's own terminology. The underlying probe (PandaPlayerStateProbe) already reads the game's LuaOriginEnergy / LuaMaxOriEnergy fields, which are the stamina pool — the rename is only the C# identifier on our side. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-phase-9a-layout-primitives-design.md.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerVitals.Health
member.summary: Current HP; zero before the player is in-world.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerVitals.MaxHealth
member.summary: Maximum HP; zero before the player is in-world.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerVitals.Stamina
member.summary: Current stamina (origin energy); zero before the player is in-world.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerVitals.MaxStamina
member.summary: Maximum stamina; zero before the player is in-world.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerLocation
member.summary: Location facet of the local player's state.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerLocation.Position
member.summary: World-space position of the local player; before the player is in-world.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerState
member.summary: Read-only view of the local player's basic state. All properties are safe to read at any time; when is false (e.g. on title / character select / loading screens) the other properties return defaults (empty string, zero, ).
member.remarks:
The service is polled — the framework refreshes the snapshot once per game tick (via Panda.Core.Game.Update). Plugins typically read the values from their own IFramework.Update handler.
v1 surface: Name, Level, HP, Stamina, Position. XP / buffs / target / class are deferred to a later phase.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerStats
member.summary:
Read-only access to live player attribute values. Each tracked attribute is identified by its Zproto.EAttrType integer code (e.g. 11011 for AttrStrengthTotal). Pair with to resolve the localized label and to discover the UI group.
Attributes must be d before they are sampled. The probe polls only subscribed IDs each Game.Update tick — sampling all 1289 EAttrType members per frame would be wasteful. Unsubscribed IDs return null.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerStats.IsAvailable
member.summary: True once a character is loaded and the entity probe is bootstrapped.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerStats.TryGetAttribute(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the latest sampled value for , or null if the ID is not subscribed or the player isn't loaded. Value type is long regardless of storage size — the probe handles int/long memoization internally.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerStats.Subscribe(System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds an attribute ID to the polled set. Idempotent.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPlayerStats.Unsubscribe(System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes an attribute ID from the polled set.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginConfig
member.summary: Per-plugin persistent configuration. Each plugin gets one JSON file on disk (<plugin-dir>/<pluginGuid>.config.json) organized into named sections. Plugins read settings on construct, write whenever the user changes them, and subscribe to to react to external edits or settings-window writes from sibling code.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginConfig.GetSection(System.String)
E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginConfig.SectionChanged
member.summary: Fired when any section's underlying file changes — either via from this process or an external edit detected by the file watcher. The string argument is the section name. Subscribers are called on the game thread.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginInventory
member.summary: Read-side surface for the Settings → Plugins panel. Lists every discovered plugin with its current enabled / errored state, fires on state changes, and lets the user request a recovery retry after an exception.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginInventory.List
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of all discovered plugins and their current state.

member.name: E:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginInventory.StatusChanged
member.summary: Fired whenever any tracked plugin's enabled/errored state changes.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IPluginInventory.RequestRetry(System.String)
member.summary: Asks the registry to clear IsErrored and re-attempt construction next frame.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IResonanceState
member.summary: Read-only view of the local player's equipped Battle Imagines (Resonance Skills), decoded from CharSerialize.resonance (wire field 28). The equipped set drives the CombatMeter's trailing Imagine icons for the self row. Populated on the network receive thread and read on the main thread; implementations publish an immutable snapshot so reads are lock-free.

member.summary: The equipped Imagine resonance ids, in slot order ([0] = left / X, [1] = right / Z). Empty until a container sync has populated it. Each id resolves to display + cooldown data via .

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IThemeAccess.ColorRegistry
member.summary: Phase 9b.5 — register plugin-owned colours (with a default per built-in preset) and read their resolved values via the returned slot handle. Register only colours a plugin owns; for a colour that should match the theme, read directly instead.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ITheme
member.summary: Plugin-facing theme façade. The single entry point plugins receive via IPluginServices.Theme; bundles the palette, draw helpers, semantic text, and layout primitives under one type. Facade-inheritance keeps the surface cohesive while letting each facet sit under the analyzer's member-count cap.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IThemeBaseColors
member.summary: Original Phase 8 colour vocabulary — preserved verbatim. Extracted into its own facet by Phase 9b so can remain at the STELLAR0005 8-member cap while gaining HUD + menu colour sub-facets.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IThemeColors
member.summary: Theme colour facade — zero declared members. Plugins access the full colour vocabulary (base, HUD, menu) through this single type; the split into + + exists so each facet stays inside the STELLAR0005 8-member cap (the analyzer counts declared members per interface; inherited members are unbounded).
member.remarks: Same façade-inheritance pattern as itself. The split is internal vocabulary — plugin code keeps using theme.Colors.Accent / theme.Colors.HudText / theme.Colors.MenuBackground as before.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.WindowRegistration
member.summary: A registered interactive window: its spec + the root element to render. (when set) replaces the title-bar text (e.g. a logo + wordmark row); is built right-aligned before the ✕ (e.g. mode/rotate toggles). Both null ⇒ the chrome draws the plain Title text. (when set) is invoked by the ✕ instead of just hiding the GameObject — wire it to the window's (false) so stays in sync (otherwise a rail/hotkey toggle needs two presses to reopen after a ✕ close).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.WindowRegistration.#ctor(Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.WindowSpec,Stellar.Abstractions.Services.HudElement,Stellar.Abstractions.Services.HudElement,Stellar.Abstractions.Services.HudElement,System.Action)
member.summary: A registered interactive window: its spec + the root element to render. (when set) replaces the title-bar text (e.g. a logo + wordmark row); is built right-aligned before the ✕ (e.g. mode/rotate toggles). Both null ⇒ the chrome draws the plain Title text. (when set) is invoked by the ✕ instead of just hiding the GameObject — wire it to the window's (false) so stays in sync (otherwise a rail/hotkey toggle needs two presses to reopen after a ✕ close).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IWindowHost
member.summary: Plugin/framework-facing toolkit: describe an interactive window as composed elements; the framework builds native uGUI and owns chrome, rendering, refresh, lifecycle, input gating, persistence.

member.summary: Registers a window AND declares a hotkey that toggles its visibility — convenience over the separate + pattern (dup-map Pattern A). The returned manages the window; the hotkey is owned by and lives for the duration of that service.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IWindowHost.Find(System.String)

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.IWindowControl.Rect
member.summary: Current on-screen rect (position + size). default until the window is mounted.

member.summary: Move/resize the window (size honoured only for windows). The new rect is persisted by the framework. Used by plugins that remember their own per-mode geometry.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData
member.summary: All data one CombatMeter row needs to paint, in framework-neutral types (no UnityEngine). The plugin computes this from its services each refresh; the framework's BuildMeterRow reproduces the bespoke borderless meter row (HP spine, class crest, name·spec·share line, role-coloured metric bar with the per-second/total overlay, self highlight, offline scrim). A plain field struct (no big constructor) so it stays clear of the analyzer's parameter/ctor-dependency caps and mirrors the plugin's former MeterRowVisual.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.Id
member.summary: Entity this row represents; default for an empty raid slot. Used by drag-to-rearrange.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.Rank
member.summary: Rank label rendered before the leader flag (e.g. "1.").

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.Name
member.summary: Character display name (e.g. "Momoko").

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.Spec
member.summary: Sub-profession label; may be empty when the spec is unknown or hidden.

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.PrimaryValue
member.summary: Primary metric string drawn on the left of the bar (e.g. per-second DPS).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.SecondaryValue
member.summary: Secondary metric string drawn on the right of the bar (e.g. total damage).

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.SharePercent
member.summary: Share-of-total percentage string (e.g. "14%").

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.RoleColor

member.name: F:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData.BarFraction

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.SocialSnapshot
member.summary: A player's on-demand social-data reply (the game's own Social.GetSocialData RPC, which the inspector triggers via the portrait path). Available for ANY player regardless of AOI proximity — the fallback source the inspector uses when the proximity broadcast is absent. Carries identity, ability score (fight point), profession, gear-by-slot, worn cosmetics and affiliation extras; NOT the secondary-stat breakdown or skills (those are AOI-broadcast only — see the design spec §2).
member.param: The player's character id (wire SocialData.char_id).
Display name from basic_data; empty if absent.
Character level from basic_data.
Ability score (int64) from user_attr_data.fight_point.
Profession id from profession_data.
Equipped items by slot from equip_data.equip_infos; never null.
Worn cosmetics from fashion_data; never null.
Guild/party/master-score/title extras; when the reply's mask excluded those sections.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.ButtonElement
member.summary: Clickable button. null = always enabled. null = the chrome's active IChromeStyle default. (poll-diffed) renders the accent/filled look when true — used for active tab / toggle-button highlighting. > 0 fixes the button width (it can't grow/shrink) and WRAPS its label — use for right-aligned cells (e.g. a hotkey binding) that must never overflow the row regardless of font metrics. (optional PNG) renders a leading icon INSIDE the button, co-centred with the label by the button's own layout (so icon↔label alignment can't drift with font metrics — used by the Settings hub's iconed tabs).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.SpacerElement
member.summary: Gap inside a Row. = 0 → flexible (expands to push following siblings to the far edge); > 0 → a fixed-width spacer (e.g. to balance a right-aligned control so a centred element is TRULY centred, not centred-minus-that-control). > 0 → a fixed-height gap (use in a Column for a little vertical margin between sections, no divider line).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.TileElement
member.summary: Icon tile (launcher): a centred PNG over an optional , no background. Hover brightens icon+label and grows the icon ~1.18× (the native rail feel). The whole tile clicks via . When != null, a ★/☆ toggle overlays the top-right corner (its own click → ). null ⇒ icon-only (title-bar mode/rotate toggles); a non-null Func ALWAYS builds the label cell (so a live label that is empty at build time — e.g. a plugin that registers later — still shows its name once available).
