member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.CellElement
member.summary: Width-controlled column cell — fixes or weights a cell's width for aligning a Row's children into a table. Wraps any subtree. > 0 fixes the cell width (cannot grow/shrink — use for numeric columns like Current / Proj / Δ so they align across the header + every body row regardless of font metrics). > 0 makes the cell GROW to share leftover row width in that proportion (the elastic label column, or master-detail panes — Weight 1 : 2 = ⅓ : ⅔). Width and Weight are mutually exclusive (Width wins if both set); both 0 = natural content size. Per-cell colour stays on the Child (e.g. ) — the cell owns geometry only. Header and body align by using the SAME cell specs on every Row. Weighted cells require the enclosing Row to be a direct child of a Column / Scroll content / window body (all force-expand width — true for every layout that needs alignment).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.CellElement.#ctor(Stellar.Abstractions.Services.HudElement,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Width-controlled column cell — fixes or weights a cell's width for aligning a Row's children into a table. Wraps any subtree. > 0 fixes the cell width (cannot grow/shrink — use for numeric columns like Current / Proj / Δ so they align across the header + every body row regardless of font metrics). > 0 makes the cell GROW to share leftover row width in that proportion (the elastic label column, or master-detail panes — Weight 1 : 2 = ⅓ : ⅔). Width and Weight are mutually exclusive (Width wins if both set); both 0 = natural content size. Per-cell colour stays on the Child (e.g. ) — the cell owns geometry only. Header and body align by using the SAME cell specs on every Row. Weighted cells require the enclosing Row to be a direct child of a Column / Scroll content / window body (all force-expand width — true for every layout that needs alignment).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.SelectableElement
member.summary: Makes any subtree clickable as a whole and tints its background by interaction state — the rich-row analog of (which only wraps a single label). For list rows that are multi-line / multi-widget (a history session, a recent-lookup entry) where a per-row button would lose the layout. Rest = transparent; hover = faint accent wash; () true (poll-diffed) = a stronger accent fill. A click anywhere on the row fires . Composes inside a List/Column like any leaf.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.AccentRowElement

The CombatMeter History/Skill table rows use this for the role-coloured stripe + share-fraction wash the IMGUI build drew (DrawRowAccent). Both Funcs are poll-diffed.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowElement
member.summary: One bespoke CombatMeter row — the borderless, role-coloured, animated meter row (HP spine + class crest + name·spec·share line + role-coloured metric bar with a per-second/total overlay + self highlight + offline scrim). The framework reproduces the custom visual that the IMGUI MeterRowView drew, so the meter keeps its distinct look (it does NOT ride the generic Row/Bar primitives). is re-pulled on the window's capped refresh (poll-diffed) — the plugin snapshots its per-combatant state so the Func never allocates. Fixed 48-px row height; width fills the row. Compose inside a .
member.param: Per-combatant row snapshot, re-pulled on the window's capped refresh.
Optional: invoked when the row is right-clicked (CombatMeter row context menu); null = no menu.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowElement.#ctor(System.Func{Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowData},System.Action)
member.summary: One bespoke CombatMeter row — the borderless, role-coloured, animated meter row (HP spine + class crest + name·spec·share line + role-coloured metric bar with a per-second/total overlay + self highlight + offline scrim). The framework reproduces the custom visual that the IMGUI MeterRowView drew, so the meter keeps its distinct look (it does NOT ride the generic Row/Bar primitives). is re-pulled on the window's capped refresh (poll-diffed) — the plugin snapshots its per-combatant state so the Func never allocates. Fixed 48-px row height; width fills the row. Compose inside a .
member.param: Per-combatant row snapshot, re-pulled on the window's capped refresh.
Optional: invoked when the row is right-clicked (CombatMeter row context menu); null = no menu.

member.name: P:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.MeterRowElement.Data

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.DragSlotElement
member.summary: Wraps a grid cell so it can be dragged onto another cell. identifies the cell (the CombatMeter uses the flat group*5+slot index). fires on the grabbed cell when it is released over a different registered cell, as OnDrop(fromKey, toKey). gates the whole interaction (e.g. leader-only); when it returns false the cell neither drags nor accepts drops. While dragging, a dimmed ghost of follows the cursor and the hovered target highlights. Renderer-neutral — no IMGUI tokens.
member.param: The cell content (e.g. a ).
Stable identifier for this cell within its drag group.
Callback invoked with (fromKey, toKey) on a valid drop.
Optional gate; null means always draggable.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.DragSlotElement.#ctor(Stellar.Abstractions.Services.HudElement,System.Int32,System.Action{System.Int32,System.Int32},System.Func{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Wraps a grid cell so it can be dragged onto another cell. identifies the cell (the CombatMeter uses the flat group*5+slot index). fires on the grabbed cell when it is released over a different registered cell, as OnDrop(fromKey, toKey). gates the whole interaction (e.g. leader-only); when it returns false the cell neither drags nor accepts drops. While dragging, a dimmed ghost of follows the cursor and the hovered target highlights. Renderer-neutral — no IMGUI tokens.
member.param: The cell content (e.g. a ).
Stable identifier for this cell within its drag group.
Callback invoked with (fromKey, toKey) on a valid drop.
Optional gate; null means always draggable.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.RenderTextureHostElement
member.summary: Hosts a live render texture in a fixed-size box (e.g. the Entity Inspector's 3D portrait). The framework binds the boxed UnityEngine.Texture returned by onto a uGUI RawImage and re-pulls it each frame, so a texture created after the window builds still appears. Renderer-neutral — the texture crosses the boundary as so Abstractions names no Unity type; null = blank box.
member.param: Supplies the boxed render texture to display (null until ready).
Box width in px.
Box height in px.
Optional: called with pointer-drag deltas (dx,dy in px) while the box is dragged — e.g. to orbit a 3D preview camera. Null = the box ignores drags.
Optional: called with the scroll-wheel delta while the cursor is over the box — e.g. to zoom a 3D preview. Null = the box ignores scrolls.
Optional: called with pointer-drag deltas (dx,dy in px) while the box is dragged with Shift held — e.g. to pan a 3D preview camera. Null = Shift+drag falls back to .
Optional: called each frame with the box's current pixel size (w,h) so a 3D preview can size its render texture + projection to the (resizable) pane — fills it with no letterbox/stretch.

When true the pane draws NO opaque backdrop behind the texture — the render texture's own alpha shows whatever is behind the pane (the window chrome), so only the rendered subject is visible. Default false = solid dark backdrop.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.GameTextureElement
member.summary: Displays a game-asset texture handle (boxed UnityEngine.Texture) at a fixed pixel size, optionally cropped to a UV sub-rect (for atlas icons such as profession crests). The framework re-pulls (and when set) each frame, so an icon whose async load finishes after the window builds still appears. Renderer-neutral — the handle crosses the boundary as . Null texture = an invisible box that keeps its layout slot. The simpler sibling of (no drag/zoom/pan, no backdrop) and of (which takes PNG bytes, not a live texture handle).
member.param: Supplies the boxed texture to display (null while loading / unavailable).
On-screen width in px.
On-screen height in px.

Optional dynamic UV sub-rect (0..1, bottom-left origin); null = full texture. Both Funcs are invoked every frame while the element is active in the hierarchy (hidden windows/tabs are skipped; a late-loaded icon binds on its first visible frame) — keep them cheap (cache lookups, no allocation).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.SpriteElement
member.summary: Sub-rect of a packed atlas PNG — the DrawTextureWithTexCoords analog. is the whole packed sheet (loaded once, mipmap-smoothed); is the normalized sub-rect to show (x,y,w,h in 0..1, origin BOTTOM-left per Unity texture space); / are the on-screen pixel size. Use for icon atlases (e.g. a stat-icon sheet) where 's whole-PNG display can't pick one glyph. is poll-friendly (re-read each build). (optional) makes the sub-rect DYNAMIC — re-pulled on the window refresh so one pooled/recycled slot can show a different atlas cell as its backing data changes (e.g. a stat-icon keyed to the attribute the row currently represents). When null, is static (set once at build) — use that for a fixed icon (e.g. a gear).

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.SpriteElement.#ctor(System.Func{System.Byte[]},Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.UvRect,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Func{Stellar.Abstractions.Domain.UvRect})
member.summary: Sub-rect of a packed atlas PNG — the DrawTextureWithTexCoords analog. is the whole packed sheet (loaded once, mipmap-smoothed); is the normalized sub-rect to show (x,y,w,h in 0..1, origin BOTTOM-left per Unity texture space); / are the on-screen pixel size. Use for icon atlases (e.g. a stat-icon sheet) where 's whole-PNG display can't pick one glyph. is poll-friendly (re-read each build). (optional) makes the sub-rect DYNAMIC — re-pulled on the window refresh so one pooled/recycled slot can show a different atlas cell as its backing data changes (e.g. a stat-icon keyed to the attribute the row currently represents). When null, is static (set once at build) — use that for a fixed icon (e.g. a gear).

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.VirtualListElement
member.summary: Scroll-windowed variable list: renders a small fixed of K rows (K ≈ viewport rows + a few margin rows) over a logical list of () items that may be FAR larger than K. The framework sizes a scroll content spacer to Count()*RowHeight, tracks the scroll offset, computes the first visible logical index, calls (first) BEFORE pulling any slot values (so the plugin's row Funcs resolve item first+slotIndex), positions the pool rows at their logical offset, and SetActive-shows only rows whose logical index < Count(). Rows MUST be uniform fixed-height (). Use for large pickers; the small (eager full pool) stays for short lists. is the viewport height.

member.name: M:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.VirtualListElement.#ctor(System.Func{System.Int32},System.Single,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Stellar.Abstractions.Services.HudElement},System.Action{System.Int32},System.Single)

Rows MUST be uniform fixed-height (). Use for large pickers; the small (eager full pool) stays for short lists. is the viewport height.

member.name: T:Stellar.Abstractions.Services.CooldownTileElement
member.summary: One cooldown/debuff tile for the CooldownBar: a fixed icon square (game-asset art via /) with an -tinted outline, a foot fill-bar (width = , 0..1), an optional ★ corner badge when , an optional ×N charge count when > 1, and a centred caption below — all poll-diffed each refresh. The framework binds the boxed texture on the window's refresh pass (renders without the runtime ticker, unlike ), so it appears immediately. Compose pooled tiles inside a wrapped in s for active-only collapse.
member.param: Supplies the boxed UnityEngine.Texture for the tile art (null = neutral box).
The atlas UV sub-rect for the art (0..1, bottom-left origin).
Foot fill-bar fraction (0..1) — typically cooldown/​debuff completion.
Remaining-time caption shown below the icon.
Tile accent — outline + fill + caption tint (e.g. cyan = cooldown, red = debuff).
When true, a ★ corner badge marks an Imagine-lockout tile.
When > 1, a ×N badge shows remaining charges.
